phase_sequence: 9 of 10 gated steps (Phase 7 of 9 in roadmap numbering)

predecessor_state.commits: 0d7cfa9..971eec0 (6 commits implementation) + 1e7f6f6 archive in CORE + 614b699 ledger in ASSISTANT
predecessor_state.test_state: 140/140 pipeline tests PASS, 21/21 element-extractor tests PASS (incl. Phase 1 piggyback regression test)
predecessor_state.chokepoint: validatePipelineState invoked exactly once in populate.ts after orchestrator.run() and before generator loop (chokepoint INVARIANT 3/3 PASS)
predecessor_state.validation_result_contract: src/pipeline/output-validator.ts exports ValidationResult with shape: { ok: boolean, errors: ValidationError[], warnings: ValidationError[], report: ValidationReport (11 numeric fields) }. Caller owns process.exit; validator is pure (no fs, no console).

purpose: Make indexing/RAG output trustworthy. Today, chunksIndexed:0 silently reports success; failed/skipped chunks lack reasons; RAG queries cannot filter by layer/capability/constraint. Phase 7 closes these gaps and operationalizes the semantic facets that Phases 1-3 established. After Phase 7, indexing refuses to run on graphs that fail Phase 6 validation, and downstream agents can trust indexing metadata.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Indexing refuses to run when validation-report.json shows ok=false. Returns top-level status='failed' with reason='upstream_validation_failed' and references the validation report path.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Unit test: stub validation-report.json with ok:false, invoke indexing-orchestrator, assert status=failed and no embedding API calls made (mock embedding service).

acceptance_criteria.statement: IndexingResult adds a top-level status field with values 'success' | 'partial' | 'failed'. 'partial' is returned when chunksFailed>0 OR chunksSkipped>0 with reasons; 'failed' is returned when chunksIndexed=0 OR upstream validation failed.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Three unit tests: (a) all chunks succeed → status=success; (b) some failed/skipped → status=partial with reasons array; (c) zero chunks → status=failed with reason='no_chunks_produced'.

acceptance_criteria.statement: IndexingResult.chunksFailed[] and chunksSkipped[] entries each carry a reason field (enum: 'unresolved_relationship' | 'header_status_missing' | 'header_status_stale' | 'header_status_partial' | 'embedding_api_error' | 'unchanged' | 'malformed_chunk'). No skip or failure is silent.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Unit test: feed chunk-converter elements with each headerStatus value + an unresolved-relationship element + a deliberately malformed chunk; assert each entry in chunksFailed/chunksSkipped has the matching reason enum value.

acceptance_criteria.statement: chunksIndexed:0 returns status='failed' with reason='no_chunks_produced'. The current behavior (success with chunksIndexed:0) is the documented anti-pattern this phase eliminates.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Integration test: run indexing on a project with no exported elements (or all elements skipped); assert IndexingResult.status='failed' and exit code non-zero from rag-index CLI.

acceptance_criteria.statement: CodeChunk type gains layer, capability, constraints, headerStatus fields (all optional). chunk-converter.ts populates them from ElementData when present.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Type-level test (TypeScript compile) + unit test on chunk-converter: supply ElementData with layer='service', capability='auth', constraints=['no-pii']; assert resulting CodeChunk carries the same values.

acceptance_criteria.statement: CodeChunkMetadata (the filter type used by query-engine.ts at line 60 'filters?: Partial<CodeChunkMetadata>') gains layer, capability, constraints fields, enabling existing filter machinery to scope queries by these facets without new code paths.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Unit test on query-engine: pass options.filters={layer:'service'}; assert vectorOptions.filter.layer is set; integration test: index a corpus with mixed layers, query with layer filter, assert returned chunks all match.

acceptance_criteria.statement: rag-search CLI gains --layer, --capability, --constraint flags that pipe through to QueryEngine.filters. Each flag is optional; absence preserves pre-Phase-7 behavior.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: CLI integration test: `rag-search --layer=service 'authenticate'` returns only chunks where layer=service; absence of flag returns all matching chunks.

acceptance_criteria.statement: rag-index CLI exit code reflects IndexingResult.status: success→0, partial→0 with stderr summary, failed→non-zero. This propagates indexing health to CI/automation.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Three CLI integration tests covering each status path; assert exit code matches.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Real-world AC: running rag-index on coderef-core's own source with the Phase 6 baseline (validation-report.json ok=true, valid_edge_count 3464, header_missing_count=262) produces IndexingResult.status='partial' with documented header_status_missing skips matching the file-grain worst-severity aggregation of validation-report.header_missing_count (Path A). Zero unexplained skips/failures.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Real-world integration test: run rag-index against coderef-core src/, assert IndexingResult contains explicit reason for every chunksSkipped entry (expected: 262 with reason='header_status_missing' matching validation-report header_missing_count under file-grain aggregation).

acceptance_criteria.statement: Boundary enforcer test no-rag-indexing.test.ts (Phase 6's enforcer asserting NO Phase 7 fields leak) is DELETED in Phase 7 — Phase 7 IS the indexing layer, the enforcer is obsolete. CREATE no-phase-8-docs-leak.test.ts asserting no Phase 8 documentation-sync fields (e.g., docsGenerated, schemaDoc, apiContractMd) leak into Phase 7 output.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: Test file existence and content: __tests__/pipeline/no-phase-8-docs-leak.test.ts must exist and assert absence of doc-sync fields in IndexingResult, ValidationReport, and ExportedGraph.

acceptance_criteria.statement: All 6 graph-ground-truth.test.ts assertions remain PASS. Phase 7 is read-only over PipelineState and ExportedGraph — any regression here is a hard halt.
acceptance_criteria.test_strategy: CI gate: graph-ground-truth.test.ts runs and passes 6/6.

out_of_scope_hard_stops: NO behavioral changes to analyzer / resolver / graph-construction / output-validator layers (Phases 1-6 are read-only territory)
NO new edge schema fields, no new resolutionStatus values, no new EdgeEvidence variants
NO new vector store backends (chroma, pinecone, sqlite already exist - work within them)
NO modifications to ElementData type beyond confirming layer/capability/constraints/headerStatus already exist on it (added in Phase 1 + Phase 2.5)
NO Phase 1 piggyback fixes (Phase 6 used its one-fix budget on element-extractor.ts:188 - no further piggybacks)
Maximum 2 new CLI flags across all of Phase 7. Each must justify its existence in DR (DR-PHASE-7-D).

src/indexer/.responsibility: Lower-level index storage and query primitives. Phase 7 likely does NOT modify this layer - the rag/ layer is the integration point.

src/integration/rag/.responsibility: RAG pipeline integration. THIS is the Phase 7 surface.

key_seams.CodeChunk type: src/integration/rag/code-chunk.ts - extend to add layer/capability/constraints/headerStatus optional fields
key_seams.ChunkConverter: src/integration/rag/chunk-converter.ts:33 convertGraph() - read ElementData facets and write to CodeChunk
key_seams.IndexingResult: src/integration/rag/indexing-orchestrator.ts:99-... interface IndexingResult { chunksIndexed, chunksSkipped, chunksFailed, ... } - add status field + reason fields on skipped/failed entries
key_seams.QueryEngine filter: src/integration/rag/semantic-search.ts:60 'filters?: Partial<CodeChunkMetadata>' - works as-is once CodeChunkMetadata gains the facet fields

src/integration/vector/.responsibility: Pluggable vector storage. Phase 7 does NOT add backends. Verify metadata filter works across all 3 backends; sqlite is current default per .coderef/rag-index.json.

src/cli/.responsibility: CLI entrypoints. rag-index.ts and rag-search.ts gain new flags; populate.ts may gain optional indexing-trigger after validation passes (DR pending).

src/pipeline/output-validator.ts.responsibility: Phase 6 validator. Phase 7 reads ValidationResult to gate indexing; does NOT modify.

boundary_enforcer_disposition.delete: __tests__/pipeline/no-rag-indexing.test.ts - Phase 6's boundary enforcer asserting Phase 7 fields (ragIndex, embeddingVector, vectorStoreId, chunkId) do NOT leak into Phase 6 output. Phase 7 IS those fields, so the enforcer is obsolete.
boundary_enforcer_disposition.create: __tests__/pipeline/no-phase-8-docs-leak.test.ts - asserts NO Phase 8 documentation-sync fields (e.g., docsGenerated, schemaDocPath, apiContractMd, generatedAt as a documentation timestamp) leak into IndexingResult, ValidationReport, or ExportedGraph. Mirrors the Phase 6 → Phase 7 boundary pattern.

phase_6_gate_contract.trigger: indexing-orchestrator.ts entry point (or rag-index.ts CLI) reads .coderef/validation-report.json before any chunk conversion
phase_6_gate_contract.refuse_condition: If validation-report.json does not exist OR ValidationResult.ok is false (errors[].length > 0 with kind in ['graph_integrity', 'phase5_demotion']), return IndexingResult{status: 'failed', reason: 'upstream_validation_failed', validationReportPath: <path>} immediately. No embedding API calls.
phase_6_gate_contract.warning_pass_through: Validation warnings (kind: 'header_drift' when --strict-headers absent) do NOT block indexing - they propagate through to chunksSkipped reasons (header_status_missing/stale/partial) at the per-element grain.
phase_6_gate_contract.rationale: Phase 6 already differentiates errors (fail-hard) from warnings (informational). Phase 7 honors that distinction: graph integrity errors block indexing entirely; header drift warnings degrade to per-chunk skip reasons.

proposed.purpose: Filter RAG search results by element layer enum. Maps to QueryEngine options.filters.layer. Optional. Absence preserves pre-Phase-7 behavior.
proposed.justification: Roadmap Phase 7 task 6 explicitly requires 'RAG queries can be scoped to a layer'. No alternative without a new flag - filter param has no other CLI surface.

proposed.purpose: Filter RAG search results by capability tag. Same pattern as --layer.
proposed.justification: Roadmap Phase 7 task 6 requires capability as a queryable facet. Co-located with --layer for symmetry.

deferred_to_phase_8_or_later: --constraint=<value> filter - capability/layer cover the primary use cases; constraints may be sparse in the corpus initially. Add only if usage demands.
--strict-validation (rag-index) - currently the gate is binary (ok/not-ok); a strict mode would also block on Phase 6 warnings. Defer until header drift is observed in a non-test corpus.

halt_and_report_to_orchestrator_during_i: If chunk-converter.ts requires changes to ElementData propagation that would touch the analyzer or pipeline-state layer (out-of-scope per hard stops). Surface as a follow-up stub instead.
If any of the 3 vector store backends (chroma/pinecone/sqlite) does not support metadata-based filtering for the new facet fields. May need an adapter shim or backend-specific filter translation.
If real-world AC-09 fails because chunksSkipped count diverges from validation-report.header_missing_count (262) - investigate but do NOT silently demote AC; halt and report.

If the boundary enforcer create-step (no-phase-8-docs-leak.test.ts) fails because Phase 8 fields ALREADY exist somewhere in the pipeline (suggests prior phase scope creep). Report and surface as a stub - Phase 7 does NOT clean up other phases.
session_routing: daily-agent-session-2026-05-04 - all Phase 7 work routes through 5-4. Phase 6 close was the last 5-2-pinned milestone.
